cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about the differences between the self-driving car developed by Oxford University and those from other companies like Audi and Ford. The question asks what makes the Oxford car different. The passage says that the Oxford car is much cheaper because it uses off-the-shelf technology and an ordinary iPad. So "off-the-shelf technology" means it's readily available and not custom-made, which makes it low-cost. Looking at the options: A) use available low-cost technology. That seems to fit with the description of off-the-shelf tech and an iPad. B) depend on GPS to understand the location. But the passage says the Oxford car doesn't use GPS; instead, it learns routes when a person is driving. So B is incorrect. C) can choose the places for their owners to visit. The text mentions the car learns routes to places the driver frequents, like work or the pub, but it doesn't say the car chooses the places. The driver would presumably choose where to go, and then the car can take over once it's learned the route. So C is probably
